headline: Survey at 6% of target; strong regional concentration risks skew results.
collection_status: 80 responses collected against 1,350 target—on pace for ~288 annual responses if current rate holds. Geographic coverage heavily weighted to Lindi (31%) and Tanga (25%); four regions under-represented. Teacher respondents dominate (39 of 80), limiting diversity of perspectives.
key_findings: Expansion appetite is clear: 35 stakeholders (44%) prioritise expanding KiuFunza, with policy integration (15) and new programmes (16) as secondary levers for scale.
Partner satisfaction high (4.7/5) but scale readiness low (2.6/5)—indicates confidence in approach but material gaps in implementation capacity or systems readiness.
Feedback themes centre on sustained teacher incentives, digital monitoring, government alignment, and replication across districts—actionable but require resourcing and policy engagement.
Volunteer cohort (18 respondents) underrepresented relative to core programme staff; limited insight into community-level barriers or enablers.
flags: Geographic imbalance: Songwe, Mtwara, and Mara contribute only 21 responses combined (26% shortfall). Risk of regional blind spots in final report.
Scale readiness gap (2.6/5) with high expansion ambition suggests stakeholders want growth but doubt systems can absorb it—test capacity constraints before scaling.
Sample skew toward teachers and school leaders; missing voices from district education officials (5 respondents) and regional coordinators (1) who control policy levers.
recommendation: Immediately surge collection in Songwe, Mtwara, Mara, and other low-response regions to meet geographic targets. Simultaneously boost DEO/REO/arc outreach—these officials are critical for policy embedding, yet represent <5% of current sample. Recalibrate timeline: at current pace, reaching 1,350 by survey close requires 3–4x collection rate.
